other_observers: Bill Schmoker, Marcel and Joel Such, Peter Gent, Bill Kaempfer, m.ob.; Found by Larry Semo and Steven Mlodinow earlier that day.
description: Scaup-sized duck with longer feathers forming a distinctive "tuft" at rear of head. The overall coloration appeared brown, with head and back darker brown than the light milk chocolate brown chest and mottled milk chocolate/whitish flanks. On the side profile photo submitted, the head appears to have a slight purplish sheen behind the eye. This would indicate a male; however, I hesitated identifying age/sex as I am unsure if male Tufted Ducks can have white undertail coverts, which is shown on another submitted photo. The bill was bluish with a substantial black tip with a less evident whitish subterminal band. Eyes were bright yellow.  I observed the bird flap several times and noted an extensive white wingbar that reached the 2nd or 3rd outermost primary. The belly was pure white becoming mottled on the lower chest then brown at the neckline. Posteriorly, the white belly faded to the milk chocolate color around the vent. The undertail coverts were white with large, brown mottling.Behaviors: Occasionally diving for extended periods (~10-20 seconds) in the small waterhole remaining of an otherwise frozen pond. Appeared to attempt a take-off on two occasions while I was there, but quickly settled back into the water without actually taking off.Call: None heard.Plumage: unknown
photographer: Photographer: Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner
